Off Calendar Aug-02-2021 Continued To Oct-04-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Rowe, via ZOOM, for Ferdinand Angeles, Administrator.

The court begins by asking counsel why he has failed to respond to the Examiner's notes. Counsel reports that there have been delays in obtaining an original birth certificate and death certificate due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Counsel also reports that money has been transferred to a Fidelity account and that he plans to file an ex parte petition to increase bond. The court and counsel then discuss the steps that he can attempt to move this matter along.

The Accounting Petition is continued to October 4, 2021, at 9: am in Department 204. The Order to Appear is taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: C. Andam. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-28-2021 Continued To Aug-02-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Administrator Ferdinand Angeles and the Attorney David Rowe to appear on August 2, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why they failed to respond to the Probate Examiner's notes. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Loo. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Closing Estate
Off Calendar Jan-27-2020 Continued To Apr-06-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Rowe appeared by telephone for Ferdinand Angeles (Administrator).

Mr. Rowe stated that the inventory and appraisal is with the Probate Referee (Michael Kasolas), that they are waiting for the inventory to do final distribution, that they found unclaimed assets in the amount of $5,700.00 and that he had medical issues. Mr. Rowe also stated that one of the Beneficiaries in the Philippines died and that he did not do a petition for approval of report on status of administration because he thought this was the proper way to proceed. The Court noted that letters were issued 16-months ago and that they were supposed to file the inventory and appraisal 4-months after appointment. Status on close of estate is continued to April 6, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration and at the least a partial inventory and appraisal needs to be filed by March 30, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Anasse. Not reported.
